top_weaknesses.issue: Manifesto-language pile-up: phrases like 'rent-seeking machine,' 'open-weight curve is bending fast,' 'cost-per-capability curve,' 'small constellation of specialized open-weight models' read as essay-ish/VC-deck and dilute the lived-builder voice
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut 10-15%; replace one or two abstractions with a concrete, specific local-stack example (a real model, a real laptop spec, a real workflow)

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice flips between angry-builder lede and analyst/policy-memo middle ('repayment vehicle is the per-token API charge,' policy bullet list at end)
top_weaknesses.fix: Hold the builder voice through; trim the policy laundry list at the close or replace it with a one-line concrete call

top_weaknesses.issue: 82-word paragraph ('The rent is on a meter you can't read...') is dense on mobile but not a wall
top_weaknesses.fix: Optional split after 'It is a service you consume, on terms you do not write.'

top_weaknesses.issue: Discovery-language gap: H2s and early body lack common query phrases ('local AI models,' 'self-hosted AI,' 'open-source LLMs')
top_weaknesses.fix: Weave one natural query phrase into an H2 or the lede

top_weaknesses.issue: DeepSeek section hedges its own punch ('isn't, for everyone')
top_weaknesses.fix: Either commit to the claim or move the caveat to a footnote

consensus_summary: Strong, share-worthy thesis with a sticky frame ('rent on cognition'); H2 outline tells the story cleanly. Held back by manifesto/analyst voice flips and a missing concrete local-stack example.
